description_long: This is a dark glass bottle with a broad, rounded body that tapers into a short, cylindrical neck topped with a domed stopper. The front of the bottle features a rectangular, aged paper label with the text 'S.S. Pierce Co. IMPORTERS AND GROCERS RUM' printed in a serif font, giving it a vintage appearance. The glass surface is glossy and shows some subtle surface irregularities and reflections, suggesting an old, hand-blown manufacturing process. The stopper appears to be made of the same dark glass as the bottle and fits snugly into the neck. The bottle's back and sides are unadorned, with the dark glass obscuring any view of the interior. The overall form and labeling suggest it was used for storing and serving rum, likely in a historical or antique urban context.
description: A vintage dark glass bottle with a domed stopper and an aged 'S.S. Pierce Co. RUM' label on the front, featuring a glossy, reflective surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing the aged paper label with 'S.S. Pierce Co. RUM' and the domed glass stopper.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the bottle's broad, rounded body and the smooth, unadorned dark glass surface.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain, glossy dark glass with no label or markings visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the bottle's profile and reflective surface.
attribute_label_or_branding: Aged paper label on the front with 'S.S. Pierce Co. IMPORTERS AND GROCERS RUM' in black serif font; label is rectangular and slightly discolored.
